headline: Orang Asli Suicide Rate 3 Times National Average
context: Health Ministry data shows the Orang Asli suicide rate was 18.4 per 100,000 in 2024 — three times the national average of 6.2 — concentrated among youth aged 15-24.

cards.big: Orang Asli youth suicide is 3 times the national average.
cards.sub: The rate among 15-24 year olds is 28.6 per 100,000 — comparable to rates among First Nations Australians.

cards.big: JAKOA's mental health budget for 200,000 Orang Asli is RM1.2M.
cards.sub: That is RM6 per person per year. MOH spends RM48 per person nationally.

cards.big: Only 4 Orang Asli-speaking counsellors serve the entire community.
cards.sub: Cultural and linguistic barriers mean conventional mental health services are inaccessible.

cards.big: Land loss, cultural displacement, and poverty are identified risk factors.
cards.sub: A 2024 USM study found suicidal ideation correlated with land dispossession more strongly than income alone.

cards.big: Suicide among dispossessed indigenous youth is not a mental health crisis. It is a dispossession crisis expressed through mental health.
Addressing Orang Asli suicide requires addressing its root causes: land rights, cultural recognition, and economic inclusion. Counselling alone treats symptoms of a structural problem.
